title: Sinoadina racemosa
intro: Sinoadina racemosa, also known as Adina racemosa, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to help in reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion. The bioactive constituents of Sinoadina racemosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sinoadina racemosa is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections, fever, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Sinoadina racemosa is classified in the following manner: It belongs to the family of flowering plants (Angiospermae) and is further classified under the order of Ericales.
